sections.title: Electrical Safety Pre Task JHA
sections.html:
Task Description: ______________________________________________________________________

Pre-Task Hazard Identification:

☐ Live electrical circuits/equipment

☐ Overhead power lines

☐ Underground utilities

☐ Wet or damp conditions

☐ Damaged cords or tools

☐ Lack of GFCI protection

☐ Inadequate lighting

☐ Confined space entry required

☐ Lockout/Tagout required

☐ Arc flash potential

Required PPE:

☐ Hard Hat (Class E)

☐ Safety Glasses

☐ Voltage Rated Gloves

☐ Leather Protectors

☐ Arc Flash Face Shield

☐ FR Clothing

☐ EH Rated Footwear

☐ Hearing Protection

Job Hazard Analysis:

Step #

Task Step Description

Potential Hazard

Hazard Control Measure

Responsible Party

1

Identify electrical circuits/equipment

Shock, electrocution

Verify circuits are de-energized using a tested voltage meter

Electrician

2

Apply Lockout/Tagout (LOTO)

Unexpected energization

Apply personal locks and tags to energy isolation devices

Authorized Employee

3

Inspect tools and cords

Shock from damaged equipment

Visually inspect all tools and cords before use; remove damaged items

All Workers

4

Work near overhead lines

Electrocution from contact

Maintain minimum 10-foot clearance; use spotter if necessary

Operator/Spotter

5

Use temporary power

Shock in wet conditions

Ensure all temporary power circuits have GFCI protection

Electrician/Foreman

6

7

8

9

10
